summary: Implemented a governed branch cleanup policy draft plus the specbridge-branch-cleanup-policy read-only evaluator. The policy remains draft-only with enforcement=none and cleanup_permission=none. The evaluator classifies local and local origin branch refs from branch inventory, records candidate classes and future gates, writes only the governed branch cleanup evaluation artifact when explicitly requested, and performs no branch cleanup or network operation.
policy_result: Within policy so far. All changed files are inside the issue-215 exclusive_write scope. No protected files, secrets, production configuration, billing, authentication, authorization, database, dependency, deployment, workflow, CI/CD security, operator decision registry, issue #194, or digital twin changes are included.
risk_result: Within bounds. The command is read-only when -OutputPath is omitted, reads local branch inventory only, performs no network calls, and the only allowed write path is the governed branch cleanup policy evaluation artifact. The feature explicitly performs no branch deletion, pruning, renaming, movement, archival, force-push, fetch, pull, cleanup apply mode, or retention enforcement.
unresolved_risks: GitHub CI is pending until branch push and PR creation.
Post-merge closure is still required after the primary PR merges.
Actual branch cleanup remains blocked until a future dedicated policy and execution contract authorize it.
merge_status: Pending PR creation, GitHub CI, and merge gate.
